items.headline: Anaheim Ducks Vegas Golden Knights Game 1 recap May 4, 2026
items.description: LAS VEGAS -- Ivan Barbashev scored the go-ahead goal at 15:02 of the third period, and Vegas Golden Knights recovered for a 3-1 victory against the Anaheim Ducks in Game 1 of the Western Conference Second Round at T-Mobile Arena on Monday.

items.headline: Taylor Hall delivers despite 'B-minus' game for Carolina Hurricanes
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. – Taylor Hall could sense he wasn’t playing his best game.
Hall still found a way to be a difference-maker for the Carolina Hurricanes, though, in their 3-2 overtime victory against the Philadelphia Flyers in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Second Round at Lenovo Center on Monday.

items.headline: Philadelphia Flyers cannot finish job in overtime loss in Game 2
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. -- "It's got to go in."
Travis Konecny had the puck on his stick, in the middle of the offensive zone, just above the face-off circles.
The Philadelphia Flyers forward was 1-on-1 with Carolina Hurricanes goalie Frederik Andersen with 4:45 remaining in overtime.

items.headline: Philadelphia Flyers Carolina Hurricanes game 2 recap May 4 2026
items.description: RALEIGH, N.C. -- Taylor Hall scored at 18:54 of overtime to lift the Carolina Hurricanes to a 3-2 win against the Philadelphia Flyers in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Second Round at Lenovo Center on Monday.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs second round schedule television results
items.description: The second round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs began Saturday with the Carolina Hurricanes defeating the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0. The second round continued on Sunday with the Colorado Avalanche defeating the Minnesota Wild 9-6.

items.headline: William Karlsson could return to Vegas Golden Knights lineup in second round
items.description: LAS VEGAS -- William Karlsson returned for the Vegas Golden Knights against the Anaheim Ducks in Game 1 of the Western Conference Second Round at T-Mobile Arena on Monday.

items.description:

Now, sure, those toddlers have hockey DNA and their best friend is a record-breaking NHL rookie, but still.

items.headline: Owen Tippett injury status update May 4 2026
items.description: RALEIGH, N.C. -- Owen Tippett did not play again for the Philadelphia Flyers in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Second Round against the Carolina Hurricanes at Lenovo Center on Monday.

items.headline: Lane Hutson rising to the occasion for Canadiens
items.description:
TAMPA -- With his nose sporting a couple of ugly blotches of dried blood, Lane Hutson looked like a young teenager who’d just been roughed up by the class bully.
Looks can be deceiving.
In the end, it was Hutson who helped beat up the Tampa Bay Lightning in the

items.headline: Minnesota Wild, Colorado Avalanche do not expect repeat in Game 2
items.description:
DENVER -- The Colorado Avalanche and Minnesota Wild have turned the page from a frenzied series opener heading into Game 2 of the Western Conference Second Round at Ball Arena on Tuesday (8 p.m. ET; ESPN, SN, TVAS, CBC).

items.headline: Rasmus Dahlin loving playoff hockey in Buffalo after long wait
items.description:
BUFFALO -- The things Rasmus Dahlin has dreamed about since arriving as a fresh-faced, soft-spoken future of the franchise in 2018 are finally happening.
The Buffalo Sabres defenseman couldn’t be happier.
For his first seven seasons, Dahlin, the No. 1 pick in the 2018 NHL Draft, has been done with

items.headline: Charlie McAvoy to have player safety hearing for actions in Boston Bruins game
items.description:
Charlie McAvoy will have an in-person hearing with the NHL Department of Player Safety on May 11.
The Boston Bruins defenseman is facing discipline for slashing Buffalo Sabres forward Zach Benson on Friday.
The incident occurred at 18:29 of the third period of the Bruins' 4-1 loss in Game 6

items.headline: Ridly Greig suspended two regular-season games for actions in Ottawa Senators game
items.description:
NEW YORK -- Ottawa Senators forward Ridly Greig has been suspended for two regular-season games, without pay, for roughing Carolina Hurricanes defenseman Sean Walker during Game 4 of the teams’ First Round series in Ottawa on Saturday, April 25, the National Hockey League’s Department of Player Safety announced today.
